# Task 02.9: Persian and Iran Relevance Suite

## Sprint Info
- **Duration:** 4 days
- **Milestone:** M1.5 - Search Quality Evaluation Baseline
- **Priority:** P0
- **Depends On:** Task 02.7
- **Blocks:** Tasks 03.1, 04.2, 06.3, 09.2, 09.5

## What You'll Build
Create a focused quality suite for Persian and Iran-local behavior so the product is evaluated against the audience it most needs to serve well.

## Daily Breakdown

### Day 1: Text and Script Cases
- [ ] Add Persian ZWNJ variants.
- [ ] Add Arabic/Persian Yeh and Kaf variants.
- [ ] Add Persian and Arabic digit variants.
- [ ] Add mixed Persian/English queries.

### Day 2: Local Intent Cases
- [ ] Add local commerce, banking, services, government, education, health, and news queries.
- [ ] Add entity queries for Iranian brands, organizations, cities, and domains.
- [ ] Add transliteration and typo cases.

### Day 3: Expected Result Rules
- [ ] Define what counts as excellent, acceptable, stale, spammy, or irrelevant for each bucket.
- [ ] Define freshness expectations for news/current-event queries.
- [ ] Define local-domain and local-entity expectations without hardcoded favoritism.

### Day 4: Reporting
- [ ] Add a Persian/Iran section to the offline evaluation report.
- [ ] Track failure classes separately from global metrics.
- [ ] Create a short monthly review checklist.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] At least 150 Persian/Iran-local queries exist.
- [ ] At least 50 queries have top-10 judgments before M3/M6 ranking work.
- [ ] Reports include Persian-specific zero-result, typo, freshness, and duplicate metrics.
- [ ] Known Persian failure classes are documented with owners.

## Required Evidence
- Persian/Iran query suite artifact.
- Bucket coverage table.
- Failure class report.
- Review checklist.
